item.ptxt: There are two types of OBD system depending on the vehicle specification. For identification, refer to Precaution for OBD System .
For Euro-OBD model, in case that MIL turns on, the malfunction(s) is detected in either ECM or TCM and it stores the trouble information (DTC, freeze flame data, etc.) in its memory.
According to the varieties of scan tool, check the trouble information as follows.
It is necessary to check both ECM and TCM separately because the trouble information of both modules cannot be read and displayed at once.
CAN communication OBD generic scan tool:
Check both ECM and TCM (A/T model) simultaneously.

s1.ptxt: Prepare CAN communication OBD generic scan tool or SUZUKI scan tool.
With ignition “OFF”, connect scan tool to DLC (1) located on underside of instrument panel at driver’s seat side.
Set ignition “ON” and confirm that MIL lights.
Read DTC and freeze frame data according to instructions displayed on scan tool and print them or write them down. Refer to scan tool operator’s manual for details.

attention3.ptxt: If communication between SUZUKI scan tool and TCM is not possible, check related items according to the following points for recovery.

item.ptxt: SUZUKI scan tool and communication cable
DLC power circuit and ground circuit
CAN communication system malfunction (“Control Module Communication Bus Off” is detected in any control module)

s1.ptxt: After completing the check, set ignition “OFF” and disconnect scan tool from DLC.
